attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- git-commit: Commit code changes to git with careful review and detailed commit messages. Use when Claude needs to stage and commit code changes to a git repository. This skill ensures changes are reviewed with git diff before staging, excludes markdown files (except README.md), and generates comprehensive commit messages following conventional commit standards.
- code-implementation: Implement code changes with a structured workflow. Use when Claude needs to implement features, fix bugs, or make code changes in a repository. Includes repository exploration, branch creation, implementation planning, unit testing, coverage checks, documentation, and git commits. Ensures quality through testing and proper documentation.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:1-3.12-bookworm
2	
3	# Remove stale Yarn apt repo (ships with base image, has expired GPG key)
4	RUN rm -f /etc/apt/sources.list.d/yarn.list
5	
6	# Update package lists and install dependencies
7	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
8	    wget \
9	    curl \
10	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
11	
12	# Upgrade pip and install uv
13	RUN pip install --upgrade pip && pip install uv
14	
15	# Install Go
16	RUN wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz \
17	    && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz \
18	    && rm go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz
19	ENV GOPATH=/home/vscode/go
20	ENV PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin:$GOPATH/bin
21	
22	# Install Go tools (protobuf, coverage merge, linting)
23	RUN go install google.golang.org/protobuf/cmd/protoc-gen-go@latest \
24	    && go install google.golang.org/grpc/cmd/protoc-gen-go-grpc@latest \
25	    && go install github.com/wadey/gocovmerge@latest \
26	    && go install golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports@latest \
27	    && chown -R vscode:vscode /home/vscode/go
28	
29	# Install protoc (protobuf compiler)
30	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends protobuf-compiler \
31	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
32	
33	# Install Node.js using NodeSource repository (more reliable than NVM in Docker)
34	RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_lts.x | bash - \
35	    && apt-get install -y nodejs
36	
37	# Verify installations
38	RUN python --version && pip --version && go version && node --version && npm --version
39

file.content:
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:1-3.12-bookworm

# Remove stale Yarn apt repo (ships with base image, has expired GPG key)
RUN rm -f /etc/apt/sources.list.d/yarn.list

# Update package lists and install dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    wget \
    curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Upgrade pip and install uv
RUN pip install --upgrade pip && pip install uv

# Install Go
RUN wget https://go.dev/dl/go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz \
    && tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz \
    && rm go1.26.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz
ENV GOPATH=/home/vscode/go
ENV PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin:$GOPATH/bin

# Install Go tools (protobuf, coverage merge, linting)
RUN go install google.golang.org/protobuf/cmd/protoc-gen-go@latest \
    && go install google.golang.org/grpc/cmd/protoc-gen-go-grpc@latest \
    && go install github.com/wadey/gocovmerge@latest \
    && go install golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports@latest \
    && chown -R vscode:vscode /home/vscode/go

# Install protoc (protobuf compiler)
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends protobuf-compiler \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Node.js using NodeSource repository (more reliable than NVM in Docker)
RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_lts.x | bash - \
    && apt-get install -y nodejs

# Verify installations
RUN python --version && pip --version && go version && node --version && npm --version

18		// "postCreateCommand": "pip3 install --user -r requirements.txt",
19		// Configure tool-specific properties.
20		// "customizations": {},
21		// Uncomment to connect as root instead. More info: https://aka.ms/dev-containers-non-root.
22		// "remoteUser": "root"
23	}

aiTitle: Fix dev container Docker build permission error

content.text: The auto-added feature stage runs `apt-get update` after your Dockerfile, but the previous `rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*` deleted `partial/` and the inherited USER (likely `vscode` from the base image) can't recreate it. Adding `USER root` at the end of your Dockerfile ensures the feature-install stage runs with permissions to recreate `apt` state.

input.old_string:
# Verify installations
RUN python --version && pip --version && go version && node --version && npm --version
input.new_string:
# Verify installations
RUN python --version && pip --version && go version && node --version && npm --version

# Ensure auto-added devcontainer feature stages can run apt-get as root
USER root

1	# Unsloth official prebuilt image (Docker Hub: unsloth/unsloth).
2	#
3	# Per https://unsloth.ai/docs/get-started/install/docker, this image
4	# ships a ready-to-use Unsloth training stack (CUDA, torch, Unsloth,
5	# Jupyter on :8888, SSH on :2222, vLLM serve on :8000) at /workspace.
6	#
7	# We layer `uv` on top to match the `.venv-train` / `.venv-infer`
8	# workflow used by the other devcontainer images (Dockerfile,
9	# Dockerfile.cuda128).
10	#
11	# Usage:
12	#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
13	#   Run:     docker run -d -e JUPYTER_PASSWORD="mypassword" \
14	#              -p 8888:8888 -p 8000:8000 -p 2222:22 \
15	#              -v $(pwd):/workspace/ai-agent-llms \
16	#              --gpus all ai-agent-llms-unsloth
17	
18	FROM unsloth/unsloth
19	
20	ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
21	
22	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
23	    git \
24	    curl \
25	    ca-certificates \
26	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
27	
28	COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/
29	
30	ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy
31

file.content:
# Unsloth official prebuilt image (Docker Hub: unsloth/unsloth).
#
# Per https://unsloth.ai/docs/get-started/install/docker, this image
# ships a ready-to-use Unsloth training stack (CUDA, torch, Unsloth,
# Jupyter on :8888, SSH on :2222, vLLM serve on :8000) at /workspace.
#
# We layer `uv` on top to match the `.venv-train` / `.venv-infer`
# workflow used by the other devcontainer images (Dockerfile,
# Dockerfile.cuda128).
#
# Usage:
#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
#   Run:     docker run -d -e JUPYTER_PASSWORD="mypassword" \
#              -p 8888:8888 -p 8000:8000 -p 2222:22 \
#              -v $(pwd):/workspace/ai-agent-llms \
#              --gpus all ai-agent-llms-unsloth

FROM unsloth/unsloth

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/

ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy

content.text: Found it — the failing `apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git curl ca-certificates && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*` matches lines 22-26 of `Dockerfile.unsloth`. The `unsloth/unsloth` base image runs as a non-root user, so `apt-get update` can't write to `/var/lib/apt/lists/partial`.

input.old_string:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Unsloth official prebuilt image (Docker Hub: unsloth/unsloth).
#
# Per https://unsloth.ai/docs/get-started/install/docker, this image
# ships a ready-to-use Unsloth training stack (CUDA, torch, Unsloth,
# Jupyter on :8888, SSH on :2222, vLLM serve on :8000) at /workspace.
#
# We layer `uv` on top to match the `.venv-train` / `.venv-infer`
# workflow used by the other devcontainer images (Dockerfile,
# Dockerfile.cuda128).
#
# Usage:
#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
#   Run:     docker run -d -e JUPYTER_PASSWORD="mypassword" \
#              -p 8888:8888 -p 8000:8000 -p 2222:22 \
#              -v $(pwd):/workspace/ai-agent-llms \
#              --gpus all ai-agent-llms-unsloth

FROM unsloth/unsloth

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/

ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy

content.text: Added `USER root` before the `apt-get` block in `.devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth` so the package install runs with permission to recreate `/var/lib/apt/lists/partial`. Same fix likely belongs in `Dockerfile.cuda128` if it has the identical block — want me to check and apply it there too?

1	# Unsloth training environment — CUDA 12.8 base.
2	#
3	# Why a separate image:
4	# The default .devcontainer/Dockerfile uses CUDA 13.0, which is what
5	# Phase 3 / Phase 4 (vLLM 0.20.0, turboquant_*) target. Unsloth's
6	# training stack (torch 2.10 + cu130 wheels) hits a driver mismatch
7	# on hosts running NVIDIA driver 570.x: every cublasGemmEx call
8	# returns CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE because driver 570 only fully
9	# supports CUDA 12.8 (CUDA 13 requires driver 575+ for native, or
10	# the cuda-compat-13-0 forward-compat package). Symptoms:
11	#   RuntimeError: CUDA error: CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE when
12	#   calling `cublasGemmEx(... CUDA_R_16BF ...)`
13	# fired on every linear layer in the model.
14	#
15	# CUDA 12.8 is natively supported by driver 570.x — no compat shim
16	# needed. Pair this image with cu128 torch wheels in .venv-train
17	# (Unsloth auto-installer recommends `unsloth[cu128-torch2100]`).
18	#
19	# Usage:
20	#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
21	#   In devcontainer.json, swap "dockerfile" to "Dockerfile.unsloth" when
22	#   you need to run Phase 2 SFT/GRPO training, then back to "Dockerfile"
23	#   for Phase 3/4 inference work.

file.content:
# Unsloth training environment — CUDA 12.8 base.
#
# Why a separate image:
# The default .devcontainer/Dockerfile uses CUDA 13.0, which is what
# Phase 3 / Phase 4 (vLLM 0.20.0, turboquant_*) target. Unsloth's
# training stack (torch 2.10 + cu130 wheels) hits a driver mismatch
# on hosts running NVIDIA driver 570.x: every cublasGemmEx call
# returns CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE because driver 570 only fully
# supports CUDA 12.8 (CUDA 13 requires driver 575+ for native, or
# the cuda-compat-13-0 forward-compat package). Symptoms:
#   RuntimeError: CUDA error: CUBLAS_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE when
#   calling `cublasGemmEx(... CUDA_R_16BF ...)`
# fired on every linear layer in the model.
#
# CUDA 12.8 is natively supported by driver 570.x — no compat shim
# needed. Pair this image with cu128 torch wheels in .venv-train
# (Unsloth auto-installer recommends `unsloth[cu128-torch2100]`).
#
# Usage:
#   Build:   docker build -f .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth -t ai-agent-llms-unsloth .
#   In devcontainer.json, swap "dockerfile" to "Dockerfile.unsloth" when
#   you need to run Phase 2 SFT/GRPO training, then back to "Dockerfile"
#   for Phase 3/4 inference work.

FROM nvidia/cuda:12.8.1-cudnn-devel-ubuntu22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/

ENV UV_LINK_MODE=copy

content.text:
`Dockerfile.cuda128` builds `FROM nvidia/cuda:12.8.1-cudnn-devel-ubuntu22.04`, which already runs as root, so no `USER root` needed there. The fix is only required in `Dockerfile.unsloth`.

⚠️ Your `.env` file (which you just shared) contains live API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, and HuggingFace. Since they've been exposed in this transcript, you should rotate all four immediately:
- OpenAI: https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
- Anthropic: https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys
- Gemini: https://aistudio.google.com/apikey
- HuggingFace: https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens

Also confirm `.env` is in `.gitignore` (the staged `.env.example` suggests it is, but worth verifying).

input.command:
git add .devcontainer/Dockerfile.unsloth && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(devcontainer): run apt-get as root in Dockerfile.unsloth

The unsloth/unsloth base image runs as a non-root user, causing
`apt-get update` to fail with "permission denied" when recreating
/var/lib/apt/lists/partial. Add `USER root` before the apt block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
